/designsystem.title: Portfolio Case Study
/designsystem.description: Creating clear, high-quality digital experiences for mobile and web
h1: Case Study: Design System Sandbox & AI-Powered QA
p: Automating Design Quality Control with Figma, Cursor, and MCP
Design System Sandbox: AI-Powered Quality Assurance
Bridging the gap between static design and production reality. How I use AI agents to validate architecture, accessibility, and component logic before handoff.

In enterprise environments, the cost of fixing a design error in production is 10x higher than fixing it in the design phase. Static Figma mockups, while beautiful, cannot simulate the complexity of a live browser environment.

To solve this, I established a "Design QA Lab"—an internal sandbox where I use AI tools (Cursor + Model Context Protocol) to instantly convert Figma definitions into working React components. This allows me to test tokens, themes, and accessibility in a real DOM environment, ensuring that what I hand off to engineering is robust and technically feasible.
Design systems often fail during the handoff because of "The Static Trap"—assumptions made in vector tools that break in code.
Theme Logic: Does the Surface/Secondary token actually work in Dark Mode?
Content Stress: What happens when a title is 3 lines long instead of 1?
Accessibility: Is the contrast ratio compliant when the component is in a Disabled state?
I needed a way to verify these "engineering truths" without distracting the core development team.
I implemented a modern "Design Ops" workflow that treats design tokens as the source of truth and code as a validation tool.
MCP (Model Context Protocol): Connecting the AI to the local codebase context.
Storybook: The visualization and testing environment.
A scalable system starts with robust logic. I organized the system using Figma Variables with a strict two-tier hierarchy:
This structure allows for seamless multi-brand support and instant Dark Mode switching, which I validate directly in the browser.
To ensure my components are "code-ready," I align Figma component properties directly with React props.
Boolean Properties: For toggling elements (hasIcon, isLoading).
When a developer inspects my file in Dev Mode, the structure mirrors the component API they will build, reducing cognitive load and interpretation errors.
This is the core value of the initiative. Instead of guessing, I prove the design works. Using the AI-generated sandbox, I run automated and manual tests:
Accessibility (a11y): The Storybook a11y addon automatically flags contrast violations and missing ARIA labels.
Responsiveness: Testing component behavior in fluid containers to ensure Auto Layout translates correctly to Flexbox/Grid.
Stress Testing: Injecting extreme data (e.g., names with 50 characters) to ensure the UI handles edge cases gracefully without breaking.

From "Pixel Perfect" to "Logic Perfect." By integrating code-based validation into the design process, I shifted the focus from purely visual delivery to architectural robustness. This approach minimizes technical debt, accelerates development cycles, and ensures that the final product scales reliably across the enterprise ecosystem.
Tools Used: Figma, Cursor AI, React, Tailwind CSS, Storybook.

/about.title: Valeria Tkacheva - Senior Product Designer
/about.description: Creating clear, high-quality digital experiences for mobile and web
h2: Where bold creativity meets refined simplicity
p: Bravana is more than a template, it’s a statement. Built for agencies, creatives, and brands who dare to stand out with bold clarity and timeless design.

Bravana is designed for visionaries who believe less is more. We strip away the noise and let your work speak with power, precision, and purpose. Every detail is refined to showcase creativity at its boldest.
To redefine how digital presence is experienced, elevating brands and creators through elegance, simplicity, and fearless design. We envision a world where creativity is not just seen, but felt.

We craft with intention. Our mission is to give agencies and creatives the tools to present their work with strength and sophistication. Bravana delivers design that’s flexible, minimal, and unapologetically confident.
We’re a small, passionate team of designers, developers, and creatives who believe in bold ideas.
Here are some quick answers to the most common questions about our services, pricing, and how we work
What’s included in the branding design service?
Our branding service covers everything from logo design and color palette to typography and visual direction. We also provide brand guidelines so you can stay consistent across platforms.
How long does a typical website project take?
Timelines vary based on the scope, but most projects take between 1 to 4 weeks. One-page sites are usually done in a week, while full multi-page builds may take a bit longer.
Can I request custom features for Framer?
Absolutely! We can implement custom animations, interactions, and even integrate third-party tools, just let us know what you need.
Are there any hidden costs beyond the listed pricing?
Nope, what you see is what you get. If you request extra features outside of the initial scope, we’ll always discuss and agree on any added costs beforehand.
Do I need to prepare anything before we start?

Yes, but we’ll guide you! Ideally, have your content (copy, images, links) ready. If you need help with content, we can support or connect you with a copywriting service. We also provide brand guidelines so you can stay consistent across platforms.
What if I need changes after the project is done?
Each package includes a round of revisions, and our Studio+ plan includes 30 days of post-launch support. Additional updates can be handled on a flexible, hourly basis.

h1: I’m Valery — a senior product designer
p: Creating clear, high-quality digital experiences for mobile and web

I’m a senior product designer specialising in mobile and web experiences. With 15+ years of experience in telecom, mental health, fintech and SaaS, I design clear, scalable and outcome-driven solutions. I’ve worked with Tele2, Sberbank, Renaissance Group and international clients on premium freelance engagements.

My strength lies in solving complex product challenges with clarity and structure. I combine UX research, systems thinking and precise visual execution to design solutions that are scalable, intuitive and aligned with business goals.
Highlights from recent product and UX projects
Psychologist Matching Flow for the Mobile App
Bridging the gap between complex logic and user needs. I deliver scalable interfaces that drive revenue, not just pixels.
I don't just draw screens; I build design systems and architectures that are easy to maintain
Trusted by Healthtech and Telecom to turn massive datasets into clear, actionable dashboards
From discovery to handoff, I provide total visibility. Clear communication and predictable delivery
Clear, structured and thoughtful digital design.
I design clear, thoughtful and scalable digital products — from mobile applications to complex web platforms. Each solution is grounded in strong UX, clean structure and precise visual execution.
I create clean, modern landing pages in Framer — with clear structure, refined visuals and smooth interactions.
I help teams clarify product logic through user journey mapping, UX strategy sessions and structured problem-solving. This brings better decisions and a more seamless experience.
